Hindu destroyer-god frozen mid-cosmic dance, four arms holding flame and drum, surrounded by ring of fire, third eye blazing, matted locks whirling.
Egyptian jackal-headed psychopomp weighing hearts on golden scales against feather of truth, wrapped in funerary linens, ankh in hand.
Norse all-father enthroned in high hall, two ravens perched on shoulders, one eye sacrificed, spear Gungnir across lap, gray-bearded and watching.
Aztec feathered-serpent deity descending pyramid steps, jade-green plumes cascading, obsidian eyes gleaming, dual nature of earth and sky embodied.
Chinese monkey-deity clutching iron staff, golden circlet binding forehead, peach of immortality tucked in robes, somersaulting through clouds mischievously.
Celtic triple-aspected war-goddess manifesting as hooded crone with blood-red cloak, flanked by ravens, standing over battlefield carnage prophetically.
Japanese sun-deity emerging radiant from cave entrance, sacred mirror in hand, casting light after self-imposed exile, restoring world's illumination.
Yoruba thunder-deity wielding double-headed axe crackling with lightning, red and white beaded crown, ram horns, dance-stepping through storm clouds powerfully.
Buddhist compassion-bodhisattva seated on lotus throne, thousand arms radiating outward each holding symbol, eleven faces seeing all suffering with mercy.
Polynesian trickster-demigod hauling islands from ocean depths with enchanted fishhook, sun-tattooed and laughing, reshaping geography through cunning strength.
Greek messenger-deity with winged sandals and petasos hat, golden caduceus staff entwined by serpents, liminal threshold-crosser between mortal and divine realms.
Norse trickster-deity caught mid-transformation, half-human half-salmon, bound by entrails, venom dripping overhead, punished yet plotting escape with sly grin.
Egyptian cat-headed protector-goddess with bronze sistrum rattle, solar disc crown, nursing kittens at feet, guardian of hearth and defender against serpents.
Hindu elephant-headed wisdom-deity seated cross-legged, broken tusk writing scripture, mouse-companion at side, round belly symbolizing cosmic abundance and knowledge.
Japanese storm-deity brandishing grass-cutting sword, wild hair streaming, standing over slain eight-headed serpent, fierce protector banished from heavenly plains.
Chinese jade-emperor enthroned on cloud palace, dragon-embroidered robes, bureaucratic scrolls floating, celestial court hierarchy reflected in ornate crown tiers.
Mayan rain-deity with goggle-eyes and fanged mask, lightning-axe raised, ceramic water vessels breaking overhead, bringing drought or deluge by temperament.
Celtic horned forest-deity cross-legged beneath ancient oak, torque around neck, stag antlers branching skyward, serpent coiled in lap, lord of beasts.
Slavic earth-goddess emerging from fertile soil, grain stalks woven through hair, rye loaves cradled in arms, embodying harvest's bounty and maternal nourishment.
Aboriginal rainbow-serpent undulating across red desert sky, scales shimmering every color, creating waterholes and rivers where body touched earth in dreamtime.
Greek sea-deity rising from waves astride hippocampus, trident commanding tides, beard dripping seaweed and pearls, earthquakes trembling at his wrath.
Norse valkyrie descending on winged horse, spear selecting slain warriors, swan-feather cloak billowing, mead-horn at belt for Valhalla's chosen heroes.
Egyptian falcon-deity with crook and flail crossed, double-crown of unified kingdoms, solar barque sailing overhead, sky-spanning protector dispensing justice.
Hindu warrior-goddess astride Bengal tiger, eight arms wielding weapons, red sari flowing, vanquishing demon-buffalo, embodiment of shakti's fierce protective power.
Buddhist temple-guardian deity trampling demons underfoot, flaming sword raised, third eye blazing, wrathful compassion protecting dharma with fierce scowling visage.
Chinese dragon-queen emerging from southern sea-foam, pearl clutched in claw, storm clouds gathering, governing ocean depths and coastal fishing communities protectively.
Maya creator-god-pair intertwined, one skeletal death-aspect one fertile life-aspect, cosmic balance embodied, birthing cosmos from primordial void together.
Celtic warrior-queen standing in chariot with scythed wheels, spear raised, blue woad patterns covering skin, rallying tribes against invaders with fierce cry.
Yoruba river-goddess adorned with brass mirrors and yellow silk, calabash pouring sweet water, peacock feathers, bringing fertility and healing love's afflictions.
Inuit sea-goddess with long black braid containing ocean's creatures, finger-joints severed becoming seals, dwelling beneath waves controlling marine bounty wrathfully.
Roman dawn-goddess with rose-fingered hands painting sky, saffron robes trailing aurora colors, dew-drops falling from hair, heralding sun's chariot daily.
Norse fire-giant standing at world's edge, flaming sword raised for Ragnarok, Muspelheim's burning king awaiting final battle prophecy.
Egyptian ibis-headed knowledge-deity with writing palette and reed, moon-disc crown, recording divine words, measuring time's passage through celestial calculations.
Hindu blue-skinned preserver-deity reclining on thousand-headed serpent, four arms holding conch and lotus, dreaming universe into cyclical existence eternally.
Japanese rice-deity attended by white fox messengers, grain sheaves cradled, sake offerings surrounding shrine, ensuring abundant harvest through sacred agricultural rituals.
Aztec sun-deity demanding sacrifice, obsidian blade gleaming, turquoise mosaic face, requiring nourishment of hearts to continue daily sky-journey across heavens.
Celtic cauldron-god with massive club and inexhaustible vessel, red-bearded and jovial, harp playing itself, embodying abundance and warrior-patron generosity.
Slavic fire-deity hammering at cosmic forge, sparks becoming stars, smoky beard singed, crafting divine weapons while tending eternal sacred hearth flames.
Polynesian volcano-goddess with molten lava flowing as hair, white dog companion, creating new islands through fiery eruptions, passionate and destruction-bringing.
Native American thunderbird spreading massive wings, lightning flashing from eyes, eagle-cry shaking mountains, bringing storm-rain to parched plains below.
Roman wine-deity crowned with grape-vine and ivy, leopards at heel, thyrsus staff dripping honey, leading ecstatic processions of frenzied celebrants.
Norse sea-goddess dragging drowned sailors to underwater hall with net, nine daughters manifesting as waves, cold beauty beneath icy northern waters.
Egyptian cobra-goddess rearing with flared hood, uraeus crown blazing, spitting fire at pharaoh's enemies, fierce protector of Lower Kingdom's sovereignty.
Hindu monkey-general kneeling with mountain of healing herbs, mace resting aside, devoted servant-warrior leaping oceans to rescue prince's brother loyally.
Japanese moon-deity descending jeweled staircase from night sky, holding elixir of immortality, rabbit companion pounding mochi, governing tides and calendar.
Aztec earth-goddess depicted as toad with gaping mouths at joints, demanding blood-sacrifice, simultaneously devouring dead and birthing life from body.
Celtic salmon of wisdom swimming in sacred pool beneath hazel trees, nine nuts of knowledge falling, sought by poet-seers for prophetic insight.
Yoruba trickster-orisha standing at crossroads with cowrie shells, pipe smoking, striped trousers, opening paths between worlds with chaotic divine mischief.
Slavic thunder-deity riding cloud-chariot wielding iron axe, oak sacred to him, pursuing serpent-demon, bringing rain to fields through sky-battle's fury.
Native American spider-woman weaving cosmic web connecting all creation, teaching basket-patterns to people, grandmother-trickster balancing chaos with order carefully.
